observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to take the following actions:
a. Coordinate with the Provincial Government of Benguet to obtain the complete documentation, including detailed project locations and other pertinent information necessary for inspection;
b. Instruct the Inspection Committee to assess the infrastructure projects to verify their existence, condition, and fair value, as required under IPSAS 17; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, upon determination of the fair value of the infrastructure assets.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 2024-006, particularly the adoption of the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold, by:
a. Reclassifying all tangible items costing below ₱50,000.00 to the appropriate semi-expendable property inventory accounts; and
b. Recognizing these items as expenses upon issuance to end-users.
2.8. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to apply the change in accounting policy retrospectively, as prescribed under Item 3.5 of COA Circular No. 2024-006, by undertaking the following actions:
a. Identify all affected PPE items acquired in CY 2024 and prior years with acquisition costs below the threshold; and
b. Adjust the books accordingly to reflect their proper classification and recognition.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Review and update the LDRRMFIP to include projects and activities charged to STF from previous years which are intended for implementation in the current year; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to correct the balance of the Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund account.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the following:
a. The Inventory Committee to annually prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System to the Audit Team; and
b. The General Services Office/Treasury Office to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card.
c. The Municipal Accountant to:
i. Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card; and
ii. Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for each road components.
4.8. We further reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and Management agreed to enjoin the concerned office heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Road Asset Management System prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008 and conduct complete inventory and regular reconciliation of accounting and property records to establish a reliable local road network account in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to closely coordinate with the concerned source agencies to reconcile the net discrepancy of ₱736,356.02 between the accounting records and confirmed balances, and adjust the balances accordingly.
We further recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Exert efforts to locate the lacking Memorandum of Agreement and/or other similar documents for the eight fund transfers with incomplete documentation; and
b. Evaluate the ₱785,056.03 dormant fund transfers, and where the project has been completed or implementation is no longer feasible, promptly return the unutilized funds to the source agencies, in accordance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Exert efforts to locate the necessary supporting documents to establish the existence and validity for claiming the receivables;
b. Exert all efforts to collect the receivables accounts from the persons responsible. If all efforts are futile, file the necessary request for write-off of dormant receivables that are deemed uncollectible, supported with completed documentation as listed in Annex 8, COA Circular No. 2023-008, provided with index tabs, for easy reference; and
c. Evaluate the credit risk of the receivables amounting to ₱773,164.00 and establish the corresponding loss allowance in accordance with IPSAS 41, to ensure the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to:
a. Strictly adhere to the prescribed timelines under the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the invitation of observers and ensure that they are given at least five (5) calendar days' notice before each procurement activity; and
b. Require that Performance Securities be posted within the prescribed 10-day period and prior to any contract signing, in compliance with Sections 37, 39, and 40 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure strict compliance with Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 by requiring Warranty Security, either through retention money or special bank guarantees, for the procurement of goods.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to:
a. Fast-track the re-alignment of the Repair of Bisaka Footbridge to other priority project/s; and
b. Closely monitor the execution of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan to ensure that programs/activities are timely implemented.
11.10. We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation and Management agreed to fast-track recruitment to appoint a full time Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Planning Development Council to ensure that PPAs included in the Annual Investment Plan are well-planned and procurement and implementation ready;
b. Require the Bids and Awards Committee to make a well-planned procurement program to provide sufficient time to implement the required PPAs promptly within a reasonable time; and
c. Fast-track the implementation of the continuing infrastructure project, else consider to re-align to other feasible and priority projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the OIC–Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of the year, in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002;
b. Refrain from requesting cash advance near the end of the year, considering the limited time available for encashment and its liquidation; and
c. Ensure that cash advance covers only the authorized purposes indicated in Item 3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002, with due consideration of the instances where payment by check is difficult, impractical or impossible.
13.10. We also recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned employee or office to process claims immediately after the completion of activity or within the prescribed cut-off date set by the Accounting Office.
13.11. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly enforce cut-off dates for submission and processing of claims.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Tourism Council the following:
a. Promote and encourage tourists to register and book their tours in the online booking platform of the Municipality;
b. Provide a separate logbook for tourists to be used by the Barangays and which is designed to include the necessary information needed such as the name/s of the tour guide; place/s to visit; time-in and time out of tourists; and if the tourists are with booking with the Tourism Council or walk-in tourists;
c. Consider providing additional manpower to the Municipal Tourism Office to help in the monitoring and coordination of the tour activities in the Municipality; and
d. Recommend for amendment of the Municipal Tourism Code to suit the actual practice of the Barangays and Municipality in operation of the tourism activities.
We further recommended and Management agreed to consider a centralized collection of the tourism fees by:
a. Encouraging payment of tourism fees by the tourists through online banking directly to the Authorized Government Depository Bank of the Municipality; and
b. Deputizing the staff of the Municipal Tourism Office to issue Official Receipts upon confirmation of the online bank payment of tourists with the Municipal Accounting Office.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal Point System to:
a. Create/prepare the GAD Agenda with the required supporting documents, copy furnish the Audit Team; and
b. Ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget are in line with the GAD Agenda.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Point System agreed to:
a. Discuss or disclose the deviation between the DILG-endorsed budgeted amount and the Sanggunian-approved budgeted amount, as well as the factors that have facilitated or hindered the implementation of the DILG-endorsed and/or LGU-approved GAD Plan and Budget, in the “Variance/Remarks” column of the GAD Accomplishment Report; and
b. Include the required supporting documents in support of the GAD Accomplishment Report as required in Item C.8.5 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and within the prescribed period to avoid imposition of surcharges/penalties, as prescribed under BIR RR No. 11-98.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Agency Authorized Officer and the Electronic Remittance File Officer to closely monitor the remittances due to GSIS as well as to ensure that reconciliation and billing issues are resolved before processing remittances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize liabilities only upon delivery of goods and/or when services are rendered and record only receivables only when there is an established or valid claim against an entity or establishment.
observations.managementAction: The Accounts Payable and Receivable were recognized for the delivered and rendered services and when there is an established valid claim respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:  Inventory Committee to include donated items in the conduct of physical count of inventories; and  Municipal Accountant to: a. Prepare necessary adjusting entries for any undistributed inventory and small tangible items to be submitted by the Municipal Health Officer, if any; and b. Prepare the adjusting entries to correct the balance of the Medical Equipment account and other pertinent accounts. We further recommended that Management instruct all concerned offices to furnish the Accounting and Treasury/General Service Office with all documents for in-kind donations received and distributed for recording purposes. We also recommended that Management develop a policy on donated items being received by the municipality and on utilization thereof, copy furnish the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: JEV No. 2024-10-002218 dated October 31,2024 was prepared to correct the balance of the Medical Equipment. Further, the undistributed inventory and small tangible items were distributed. Further, the documents for the donated drugs and medicines other offices received were not being furnished to the Treasury/General Service Office. The document/s pertaining to donations received by the Municipality other offices are being furnished to the Treasury/General Service Office. The management had prepared the initial draft on the policy on donated items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their records, validate the 20 per cent share withheld under the “Other Payables” under the account, and make necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the five per cent DPWH share and the 15 per cent OBO share. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to record the 15 per cent OBO and five per cent DPWH share at least by the following amounts, and subsequently by the remaining “other payables accounts” pertinent to the 20%, once established. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Adopt the prescribed percentages in computing the OBO and DWPH shares in their reports; b. Immediately remit to the Bureau of Treasury the five per cent share of the DPWH, once established; and c. Regularly monitor collections of income from building permits to ensure timely remittance of the DPWH share.
observations.managementAction: The Accounts Payable and Other Payable account was reconciled. JEV Nos. 2024-04-000601 and 2024-04-000602 were prepared to adjust the Other Payables Account. The reconciled balance of ₱96,026.58 for the 5 per cent share of the DPWH was remitted to the BTr under check number 1405575 (JEV-2024-07-001233 dated July 1, 2024). Collections of income from building permits are regularly monitored by the Treasury Office. The prescribed percentages in computing the OBO and DWPH shares were adopted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management coordinate with the different source agencies to verify the utilization and status of the fund transfers and locate the related documents. Otherwise, return the fund transfers to the source agency if no longer implementable. We further recommended that Management settle the unliquidated fund transfer amounting to ₱1,680.00, copy furnish the Audit Team with the Fund Utilization Report. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to: a. Provide details of PPAs per fund transfer per source agency; and b. Consider the following adjusting journal entry to correct the balance of the Due to NGAs account and other pertinent account.
observations.managementAction: The MOA for the two fund transfers from the Department of Agriculture amounting to ₱295,400.00 and ₱86,000.00 were furnished to the Audit Team. The unliquidated fund transfer amounting to ₱1,680.00 was refunded thru OR No. 16919677 dated May 8, 2024. Details of PPAs per fund transfer per source agency were provided. JEV No. 2024-08-000112 dated August 30, 2024 was prepared to correct the balance of the Due to NGAs account and other pertinent accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to include the Continuing Appropriations in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts to provide complete information to the users of the Financial Statement.
observations.managementAction: The Continuing Appropriations were presented in the SCBAA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:  Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee to reconcile the LRN items that have different costs between accounting and property records;  Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for LRN projects;  Municipal Treasurer/General Service Office to prepare and maintain LRNPC; and  Inventory Committee to properly fill up the RPCLRN. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to: a. Verify the LRN items that are included in the RPCLRN or property records but not in the accounting records; b. Adopt the prescribed format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008 in preparing the disclosure of the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statement; c. Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card; d. Maintain SLs for the road components; and e. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, if warranted. We further recommended that the Management enjoin the concerned office heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Road Asset Management System prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008 and conduct complete inventory and regular reconciliation of accounting and property records to establish a reliable local road network account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Items with different costs between the GL and Inventory Schedule were reconciled and correcting entries were prepared. The RPCLRN will be filled up properly. The Local Road Network Property Card is being prepared and maintained by the Treasury Office. Local Road Network Ledger Card is being prepared. Subsidiary Ledgers for each road component are being prepared. JEV Nos. 2024-04-000729 and 2024-04-000051 were prepared to correct the balance of the LRN. The prescribed format was followed in preparing the disclosure of the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statement. The Inventory Committee will properly fill up the RPCLRN. Reiterated in Part II of this report: prepare and maintain LRNPC, LRNLC and SLs for road components.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct:  The General Service Office/Municipal Treasury Office to update, prepare, and maintain property cards for each type of asset; and  The Municipal Treasury Office and the Inventory Committee exert utmost efforts to: a. Conduct a physical count of the PPE items that are not included in the RPCPPE amounting to ₱143,174,254.95; b. Locate the properties amounting to ₱109,158.00 that were not returned or surrendered by the Accountable Officers and account for the unreturned items, if any; c. Locate the properties amounting ₱66,219.00 and account for the missing items, if any; and d. Secure property transfer documents for the items donated amounting to ₱52,500.00. We also recommended the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted; determine the fair market value of the motor vehicle donated by the DA at the date of acquisition and its estimated useful life, and furnish the Accounting Office with the appraisal report; and prepare necessary adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The RPCPPE was prepared and submitted to the Audit Team. The cost of motor vehicle donated by the DA was adjusted thru GSIS Insurance policy adopted by the management. JEV-2024-04-000600 dated April 15, 2024 was prepared to adjust the motor vehicle. The Municipal Treasury Office will update Property Cards for each type of asset. The Inventory Committee and GSO/Treasury will exert utmost efforts to conduct physical count, locate unreturned items, locate missing items and secure property transfer documents. The Manager is conducting investigations as to whereabouts of PPE items not surrendered. The property cards were not updated due to lack of human resources; management is addressing the staffing issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct:  The General Service Office/Municipal Treasury Office to update, prepare, and maintain stock cards for each type of inventory;  The Municipal Treasury Office and the Inventory Committee exert utmost efforts to: a. conduct physical count for all inventory items; b. accomplish the RPCI correctly; c. fill up the RIS properly and prepare the corresponding SSMI;  The Municipal Accounting Office to: a. analyze the abnormal balances in the Inventory Schedule; b. reconcile the variance between the GL and Inventory Schedule; and c. prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted.  The Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to regularly reconcile the RPCI and other property records with the books of accounts; and, make necessary adjustments or corrections, if warranted, for the fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statement.  All Offices to promptly submit monthly RIS appropriately summarized in SSMI for all issued or distributed inventory items to the Accounting Office for proper recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The MTO is preparing the RPCI and SSMI and submitting to the Audit Team. The MTO prepared and submitted the necessary adjusting entries. The variance between GL and Inventory Schedule was investigated and adjusting entries (JEV No. 2024-09-001965 dated September 30, 2024) were prepared to adjust abnormal balances. Stock cards are being updated. The Municipal Accounting Office and Supply Officer will regularly reconcile RPCI and property records with the books of accounts. All offices are required to submit monthly RIS summarized into SSMI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to submit the prepared certified list of taxpayers showing the amount of tax due and collectibles for the year the same to the Municipal Accountant as the basis in the recognition of Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year pursuant to Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS; and b. Municipal Accounting Office to maintain SLs of all taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had prepared certified list of taxpayers showing the amount of tax due and collectibles for the year and submitted the same to the Municipal Accountant as the basis in the recognition of Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable. The Accounting Office is maintaining a file of individual taxpayers with their respective tax dues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management institute the following actions: a. Request certificates of registration from the concerned offices (SEC, CDA, DOLE, etc., whichever is the applicable), and confirm the status of subject NGOs/POs, with pertinent details such as dates of incorporation and/or dissolution and names of incorporators; b. Issue demand letter to BABOSCAP Irrigators Association for the immediate submission of the Fund Utilization Report; c. Exert utmost effort to locate the MOA and other necessary supporting documents to establish the existence and validity of the receivables; and d. File the necessary request for the write-off of dormant receivables that are deemed uncollectible once dormancy is established, thus, supported with complete documentation listed in Annex 8 of COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: Management has requested certification on the status of the three (3) NGO/POs from DOLE and SEC. The Management received a letter from DOLE as that the Kayapa Sericulture was non-operation since September 2017. Demand letter to BABOSCAP Irrigators Association for the submission of the Fund Utilization Report was issued in July 2024. The MOA for the NGO/POs were submitted to the Audit Team. The dormancy of receivables that are deemed uncollectible are not yet established; hence, no request for write-off was filed to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit collections intact the next banking day or as the earliest possible time. We further recommended that Management fast-track recruitment to fill up the vacant positions of the Treasury Office.
observations.managementAction: The collections amounting to ₱11,638,912.58 as of December 31, 2024, was deposited on January 2025. The vacant positions were filled-up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC, BAC TWG, and BAC Secretariat to strictly follow the guidelines in inviting observers, conducting bid evaluation, and post-qualification using the criteria provided in RA 9184 so that contracts will be awarded to the most qualified bidders. We further recommended that the BAC: a. Submit a written explanation on the Invitation to the NGO representative for post-qualification received by the latter before the scheduled bid opening; and b. Submit the lacking documents.
observations.managementAction: The guidelines for inviting observers were followed. The BAC provided a written explanation on the Invitation to the NGO representative for post-qualification in June 2024. The lacking documents were submitted to the Audit Team on June 20, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC, BAC TWG, and BAC Secretariat to strictly follow the guidelines in the conduct of negotiated procurement and in inviting observers. We further recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee to: a. Include PCCI as one of the observers for the procurement of goods, if applicable; b. Conduct mandatory review for the reason/s for the failure of bidding; and c. Invite at least (3) three suppliers if management resorted to Negotiated Procurement due to two failed biddings.
observations.managementAction: The guidelines for inviting observers were being followed. PCCI has no physical office in Baguio-Benguet; however, BAC prepared invitations for PCCI. The BAC conducted mandatory review for reasons of failure of bidding. Negotiated procurement was resorted to due to two failed biddings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the following: a. End-user to properly fill up the Purchase Request with complete characteristics and/or performance requirements of the item being requested to ensure the reasonableness of the cost and item being procured and attach the sample design, if applicable; b. The canvassers require the prospective bidders to indicate detailed items, including delivery period in their accomplished Request for Quotations; c. General Service Officer/OIC – Municipal Treasurer ensures that all POs contain complete item specifications pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010; and d. Inspection Committee to properly fill up the Acceptance and Inspection Report.
observations.managementAction: Purchase Requests were reviewed and revised to include complete specifications and sample designs where applicable. Prospective bidders were required to indicate delivery period. The MTO/General Services ensured POs contain complete item specifications. Acceptance and Inspection Reports are being properly filled-up by Inspection Committee; however some reviewed Acceptance and Inspection Reports lacked information on Date Received and Date Inspected which is being addressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the concerned supplier to pay the amount of ₱68,985.00 representing the supposedly imposed liquidated damages. Else, instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to deduct such amount from any money due or which may become due to the supplier; and b. Ensure that extensions granted are carefully reviewed and follow the limit provided in the Generic Procurement Manual.
observations.managementAction: The concerned supplier paid ₱35,000.00. The Management issued demand for the remaining payment and collected ₱20,000.00 through OR No. 9139607; additionally ₱15,000.00 was collected in April 2025. Management will ensure that extensions are carefully reviewed and follow the limit provided in the Generic Procurement Manual.
observations.reason: Outstanding balance for liquidated damages being pursued; partial collections made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management indicate only the relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements in the Purchase Request so as not to effectively referred to a particular brand.
observations.managementAction: Of the reviewed Purchase Requests, the characteristics and/or performance requirements of the item referenced to a particular brand were not present; Purchase Requests were adjusted to indicate only the relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Closely monitor and facilitate the completion of the unimplemented development projects; b. Require the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to ensure that PPAs included in the Annual Investment Plan are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready; and c. Require the Municipal Engineer and MPDC to strictly monitor the progress of works and facilitate the timely implementation of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Of the development projects programmed in CY 2023, some were implemented in CY 2024. Thirteen (13) development projects were implemented. There remain some projects carried forward due to permits/waiver of rights and road-right-of-way issues. The Municipal Planning and Development Council ensured PPAs in the AIP are planned and procurement-ready. The Municipal Engineer and MPDC are monitoring progress; some projects still on-going or for re-alignment.
observations.reason: Delays due to road-right-of-way issues, securing waiver of rights from lot owners, late procurement activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to: a. Fast-track the implementation of the four (4) continuing Plans/Programs/Activities (PPAs); b. Closely monitor the execution of the MDRRM Plan to ensure that programs/activities are timely implemented in addressing projected climate risks and enhancing disaster preparedness capabilities at the LGU level; and c. Develop a well-planned procurement program to provide sufficient time to procure and/or implement the required Plans/Programs/Activities (PPAs) promptly within a reasonable time. We further recommended that Management fast-track recruitment to appoint a permanent MDRRMO.
observations.managementAction: The four continuing PPAs were implemented in CY 2024. The MDRR Plan execution is being closely monitored; some PPAs were implemented while others were delayed due to budgetary constraints and weather disturbances. The position for Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer was filled; LDRR Officer was appointed and took oath on October 13, 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accountable Officers to: a. Surrender the accessories of the returned assets in CY 2023 to the General Service Office; b. Ensure that the Property Return Slip is properly filled-up; and exercise due care in the usage and proper maintenance of the property issued to them. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer henceforth: a. See to it that returned assets are with complete accessories, otherwise, charge the accountable officer accordingly; b. Ensure that assets are properly tagged for easier monitoring; c. In coordination with the Disposal Committee, to inspect returned unserviceable assets before approving the Property Return Slip; and d. Prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of the Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and initiate the conduct of disposal of unserviceable assets. We further recommended that Management comply with the disposal procedures as provided in COA Circular 92-386 and Section 125 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Returned assets in CY 2023 were returned to the GSO. Accessories were returned. Some PRS were not properly filled-up; Management instructed employees to properly fill PRS. Inventory tags for newly procured PPEs were not prepared and old tags not updated; GSO will tag items. Disposal Committee will inspect returned PPEs before approving PRS. IIRUP to be prepared; disposal processes initiated. Disposal in CY 2024 was not completed; process ongoing.
observations.reason: Disposal process ongoing; some PRS incomplete; tagging and IIRUP preparation in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GFPS to: a. Create/prepare the GAD Agenda, copy furnish the Audit Team; b. Ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment are in line with the GAD Agenda, supported with pertinent documents; c. Make certain that gender issues are apparent by evidence such as sex-disaggregated data and that identified Gender Issues and GAD Mandate are reflected in PPAs and Objectives; d. Ensure that any deviation from the identified gender issues, activities, results and/or the targets in the GPB, the reasons for the deviations as well as the factors that have facilitated or hindered the implementation are discussed in the "Variance/Remarks" column of the GAD Accomplishment Report; and e. Inform the Local Chief Executive of any changes during the adoption of the DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget to the GAD Accomplishment Report for notification of the DILG Regional Office. We further recommended that Management judiciously prepare the GAD Plan and Budget so that the budgeted amounts and PPAs are implementable and realistic.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Agenda was prepared in CY 2024. The GAD Plan and Budget were aligned with the GAD Agenda. Variance/Remarks portion of GAD AR were filled up including reasons for deviations. Most PPAs were implemented; 5 PPAs were not implemented. Management will continue to ensure alignment and include required supporting documents. Changes during adoption of DILG-approved GAD Plan were documented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly implement the controls and monitoring systems for the timely adjustment of all valid reconciling items to correctly present the balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA account at any reporting period.
observations.managementAction: Reconciling items were adjusted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted intact and on time to avoid surcharges/penalties as prescribed in BIR RR No. 11-98.
observations.managementAction: Amounts remitted per month do not tally with those withheld for any given month; Management has initiated corrective measures. As of October 31, 2025, BIR showed a balance of ₱380,246.20 which the Municipality remitted ₱344,770.57 on November 10, 2025 leaving a balance of ₱35,475.63.
observations.reason: Under/over remittances persisted; corrective actions underway and partial remittances made after period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast-track the remittance of the balances of Due to GSIS and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts amounting to ₱77,675.56 and ₱1,400.00, respectively. We also recommended that Management closely monitor the monthly balances of the Due to GSIS and Pag-IBIG accounts for timely corrective actions to avoid penalties or sanctions prescribed in the RIRR of RA No. 8291 and RIRR of RA No. 9679.
observations.managementAction: The Due to GSIS amounting to ₱77,675.56 was reconciled and remitted from February to July 2024. The Due to Pag-IBIG amounting to ₱1,000.00 was reconciled and remitted in May 2024 while ₱400.00 could not be remitted as the employee was no longer with the Municipality thus, the amount was reclassified to Accounts Payable. Instances of under/over remittances/adjustments were noted due to differences between GSIS electronic billings and actual deductions; AAO and ERF Officer to coordinate closely with GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere to the mandated use of government plates and markings and direct the Municipal Treasury Office to mark all Municipal-owned vehicles with the marking “For Official Use Only” (3 inches), and the name of the Municipality Government is written beneath it.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adopted a new logo. The Management will facilitate the proper markings of all vehicles. The Municipal logo was applied; the marking "For Official Use Only" will be applied to the municipality vehicles.

observations.recommendation: Drivers/passengers requesting fuel/gasoline to prepare and properly fill up driver’s trip tickets; General Services Office to facilitate repair of defective speedometers to provide control in the use of vehicles and consumption of fuel and oil.
observations.managementAction: DTTs are being properly accomplished by the drivers and passengers. Some DTTs reviewed lacked the distance traveled data. Repair of defective speedometers was undertaken where possible; some speedometers were beyond repair and replacement/repair is being facilitated.
observations.reason: Some DTTs lacked data on distance travelled; repair/functionalization of speedometers ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management consider formulating a policy on service vehicles that exceeded the 30 percent allowable/reasonable limit of repairs and maintenance expense as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-003. The policy must require disposal of service vehicles that exceeded the allowable limit through public auction.
observations.managementAction: Drivers are conducting minor repairs to minimize costs. Repair of vehicles and disposal of unserviceable vehicles is being considered. Disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles was not yet conducted in CY 2024. Management is considering the enactment of a policy governing repairs of service vehicles.
observations.reason: Disposal of unserviceable vehicles not yet conducted; policy under consideration/second reading at SBO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the members of the SB to fully refund the amount of overpayment made that should have been deducted from their RATA due to absences and failure to have the required number of sessions of at least four in a month as provided for in Section 52 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

1.10. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize
liabilities only upon delivery of goods and/or when services are rendered and
record receivables only when there is an established valid claim against an
entity or establishment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording cash advances as liquidated if no actual disbursement was made by the Accountable Officers.

We further recommended that the LCE remind the Municipal Accountant of possible sanctions on cases of misrepresentation.

We recommended and Management agreed to enjoin the concerned office heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Road Asset Management System prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008 in order to come up with a reliable local road network account in the financial statements.

We further recommended that the accountant adopt the prescribed format of disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements per COA Circular No. 2015-008 and make the necessary corrections of the amount of LRN presented in the Notes to Financial Statements in order to reconcile with the amount stated in the Statement of Financial Position.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation to facilitate the disposal procedures for unserviceable property and equipment in accordance with COA Handbook on Property and Management System and observe due care and proper maintenance of equipment.

We further recommended and Management agreed to require the members of Inspection Committee to inspect and certify returned unserviceable property and equipment before issuing Property Return Slip to the Accountable Officer.

We recommended and Management agreed to consider the following:
(a) Conduct proper validation of identified or proposed projects to ensure that only feasible PPAs are included in the AIP;

(b) Instruct the concerned Municipal Officials to closely monitor the implementation of the identified development projects especially those from previous years’ budgets; and

(c) Set a timetable within which to start and complete the unimplemented projects.

We recommended and Management agreed through the LDRRMO to fast track the utilization of the LDRRMF through re-alignment of the unutilized fund and update the LDRMF Investment Plan to include priority projects to facilitate timely implementation and maximize the utilization of the fund so that benefits could be derived therefrom.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
(a) Reconcile the number of intended beneficiaries against the number of beneficiaries per distribution lists; and

(b) Indicate the date of distribution, itemize and quantify the assorted goods distributed to the beneficiaries to fully account for the procured food items.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
(a) Acknowledge all cash donations through the issuance of an official receipt and deposit the same with an Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) of the Municipality; and

(b) Submit a Liquidation Report with supporting documents to fully account the amount of ₱20,000.00 received as cash donation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
(a) Submit the Acknowledgement Receipt and Issuance Form or proof of receipt by and distribution to beneficiaries including donations that were not included in the summary list;

(b) Include in the summary list of donations the quantity of items distributed and the balances of undistributed donations in kind; and

(c) Submit inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any to establish accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
(a) Make representation to the DOE and Generation Companies (GenCos) for the possibility of submitting new Annual Work Program for CY 2021 for unutilized funds; and

(b) Fast track the implementation/completion of the remaining projects in order for the general public to derive benefits therefrom and to fully achieve the objectives of ER 1-94 Fund.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the Work Program and Memorandum Agreement between DOLE and Municipality of Bakun to the audit team to verify if the project was implemented accordingly.

We recommended that Management require the PESO Designate and MSWDO to:
(a) provide written explanation why the total amount received by the beneficiaries and dependents exceeded the maximum amount from various social amelioration program; and

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the beneficiaries to refund the excess amount received by them.

We recommended and Management agreed to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs and projects or realignment to COVID related expenses so that benefits could be derived from the budgeted amount.

observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommend the following and management agreed:
1.7.1. That in the conduct of an annual inventory of the local roads
within the Municipality’s jurisdiction, observe the accounting
and reporting guidelines prescribed by COA Circular 2015-008
by:
1.7.1.1. Proper documentation through the preparation of all
the necessary reports with the details and descriptions
of each report provided in the Annexes of said Circular;
1.7.1.2. Reconciling all the reports prepared and, thereafter,
with the record of the accounting; and,
1.7.1.3. Preparing a disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements with regards to the LRN account.
1.7.2. For the Municipality to prepare a lapsing schedule for the
computation of the depreciation of each depreciable local road
network component that will be sourced from the reports stated
earlier; and,
1.7.3. For the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the department heads’
compliance with the requirements of the said circular.

2.6. We recommended the following:
2.6.1. For the Municipality to:
2.6.1.1. formulate or update standardized policies for collection,
giving incentives or reliefs to ensure or encourage
prompt payment of RPT and SET receivables, if
warranted;
2.6.1.2. assess the probability of collection of the delinquent
taxes and consider dropping those with nil probability
in order to present a fair receivable balances in the
financial statements.
2.6.2. For the Municipal Treasurer to:
2.6.2.1. determine persons responsible for the payment of the
delinquent tax receivables from a Certified List of Tax
Delinquents and enforce collections from whom they
are established to be due from; and,
2.6.2.2. update the RPTAR based on actual and high
probability of collections to be used in setting up the
receivable accounts at the beginning of the year.

3.6. Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to the following:
3.6.1. For the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer, in
the preparation of the reports to document the LDRRMF
transactions, abide by and observe the reporting guidelines of
COA Circular No. 2012-002;
3.6.2. For the Municipal Accountant to:
3.6.2.1. take the necessary accounting entries to record the
deficiency in the transferred amount in the General and
Trust Fund books;
3.6.2.2. include in the Notes to Financial Statements, for the
following years, a disclosure of the unutilized balance of
LDRRMF in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-
002; and,
3.6.2.3. ensure that all entries in the Financial Statements
reconcile with their supporting reports and records.

4.10. We recommended management the following:
4.10.1. Submit the complete supporting documents of all the obligated
payables at the end of CY 2018 totaling ₱3.084 million; and
4.10.2. Ensure that all entries in the books of accounts of the
Municipality are supported with vouchers together with all the
necessary supporting documents.

5.9. We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to ensure the
accurate and fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial
statements by reconciling the year-end report of the Engineering Office
with their records so as not to miss out any obligations at the end of the
year.

6.6. We recommended and the Municipality agreed to:
6.6.1. Provide, in every contract, a provision on liquidated damages, in
accordance with the requirement of the Revised IRR of R.A. No.
9184;
6.6.2. Conduct project inspection at 95 percent completion, as
suggested by the Revised IRR, considering its practicability, or,
at the target project completion date when such was not yet
achieved, to determine actual accomplishment to come up with a
reliable and accurate amount of delay; and,
6.6.3. Observe strictly the guidelines in the implementation of
infrastructure contracts stated in the Revised IRR, taking
emphasis on the computation of target and revised completion
dates so as not to incur loss on the failure to bill liquidated
damages when justified by the condition.

7.6. We recommended and the Municipality agreed to:
7.6.1. strictly implement the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 by

observations.managementAction: In view of a series of legal opinions issued by the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) that local budgets take effect not upon review of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan (SP), but rather in accordance with Section 320 of the Local Government Code, which is the date of its approval or on the date fixed therein, management relied thereon. observations.reason: Considering that the supplemental appropriation ordinance had been subsequently approved by the SP concerned after end of the year, the issue on the disbursement had been moot and academic. observations.recommendation: We have recommended The Municipality and the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to seek clarification with the Bureau of Treasury (BoT) and the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) on the bond requirement before assuming collection tasks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer complied with the recommendation. By virtue of the decision of the Office of the Ombudsman in the case OMB-C-A-11-0391-G, the Municipal Treasurer was dismissed. Further, copies of the approved bonds of the Municipal Treasurer and all personnel assuming collection tasks were submitted to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to attach DVs and supporting documents when forwarding the RCIs to the Accounting Unit on a timely basis.
observations.managementAction: complied with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Verification of the bank reconciliation submitted by the Municipality did not show any unrecorded disbursements at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality:
a) Appropriate and obligate only PPAs under QRF in accordance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1; and,
b) Charge the obligated PPAs against the 70% LDRRMF allocation.
observations.managementAction:
Recommendation was complied with. Review of the Report of Utilization of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) shows that no expenditure for PPAs in the nature other than for quick/emergency purposes has been included.

The PPAs stated in the AOM were implemented after the Declaration of State of Calamity by the Provincial DRRMO as these were considered the actual and immediate need of the affected communities. Hence, were deemed appropriately charged against the 30% LDRRMF allocation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of existing rules on the use of SEF only for priority PPAs benefitting to elementary and secondary public school students.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adhered to the recommendation.
observations.reason: Review of the funds transactions under the SEF showed that no non-priority expenses were charged against said fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipality agreed to fast track the completion of these projects so that benefits from these projects will be enjoyed by the elementary and secondary public school students in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Some of the projects had already been implemented. All the projects were targeted to be completed by the end of the year.
observations.reason: Some projects were on status of on-going, for posting, and for post qualification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to:
a) Evaluate whether the unimplemented programs, projects and activities with unutilized funds of ₱2,848,251.00 are still priority concerns or had become irrelevant, taking into consideration current developments and to re-appropriate the corresponding funds to current priority undertakings; and,
b) Re-appropriate the savings from completed projects amounting to ₱78,466.78 to other priority programs and projects.
observations.managementAction:
The projects were all implemented by the end of 2016.

The amount was re-appropriated under Supplemental Budget No. 1, s. 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the accounting office:
a) Submit all disbursement vouchers to the audit team with complete supporting documents; and,
b) Ensure that documentary attachments are complete before transactions are recognized in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Management complied with the recommendation. Management submitted the following documents:
1. Resolution with the criteria for grant of CNA - SB Res. No. 155-2015
2. Comparative Statement of Approved Level of operating expenses,
3. Certificate issued by the Head of Agency of savings generated from cost-cutting measures of the Municipality –
a. Certificate of Source of Fund available for appropriation
b. Balances as of December 18, 2015
c. Report on the Payment of CNA incentive
4. Appropriation Ordinance No. 02-2015

Management will observe that in the transactions for CY 2016, no disbursements were made for CNA incentives, documents are complete before they are recognized in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipality agreed to charge expenditures to appropriate budgetary line items.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to the following:
a) Comply strictly with the provisions of Municipal Ordinance 03-2005 on the scholarship grants; and,
b) Revisit the provisions of the Municipal Ordinance to evaluate its practicability, and, if applicable, amend and revise its provisions which shall include, among other, the following:
i. Number of scholarship/financial assistance grantees; ii. Total amount allocated for financial assistance, and, iii. The source fund from 20% MDF to GF.
observations.managementAction:
Ordinance 03-2005 was amended. The provisions of the amended ordinance were strictly complied with.

The provisions of the ordinance were revisited. Amendments were introduced and Municipal Ordinance No. 06-2016 dated June 29, 2016 was passed to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipality agreed to:
a) Reclassify the payment to the students from Scholarship Grants/Expenses to Donations account; and,
b) Apportion a budget for Donations to cover support or financial assistance to these students apart from the budget for scholarship grants/expenses for the municipal employees; or, augment the budget for Donations from the Scholarship Grants/Expenses budget.
observations.managementAction:
Payment was reclassified to Donations as recommended.

The Municipality adhered to the recommendation. Verification of the charging of the scholarship assistance granted to students confirmed that these were charged against Donations account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a) Take full cognizance of their responsibility and accountability relative to the prudent use of scarce financial resources, strict and non-related personnel compliance with existing budgeting, accounting and auditing rules and regulations specifically inviting attention to COA Circular No. 85-55-A dated September 8, 1985, (updated by COA Circular No. 2012-003).
b) Conduct training needs assessment to satisfy job-specific needs and the necessity of attending trainings, seminars, conventions and the like before granting attendance to trainings/conventions; and,
c) Ensure that each and every officer/employee travelling/reporting outside the official station is granted official travel order and travelling/reporting was properly documented with reimbursement vouchers attached to vouchers upon payment/reimbursement of expenses.
observations.managementAction:
Municipality adhered to the recommendation. Review of the transactions of the Municipality showed that non-related personnel attended the CY 2016 ATOP convention.

Recommendation will be implemented in 2018. No training needs assessment was conducted.

Vouchers for the reimbursement of travelling expenses were properly documented with travel orders.
observations.reason:
Review of the transactions showed that non-related personnel attended the CY 2016 ATOP convention.

No training needs assessment was conducted. Implementation of the recommendation is expected in 2018.

Vouchers for the reimbursement of travelling expenses were properly documented with travel orders.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRMO prepare on a monthly basis the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submitted to the office concerned following the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Monthly utilization of DRRMF was prepared and submitted quarterly to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Utilization of the DRRM funds was not submitted monthly but quarterly to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended for the Sangguniang Bayan to enact the appropriate ordinance to create the Municipal DRRMO including the allocation of necessary staffing/personnel and budget.
observations.managementAction: MDRRMO position was created by virtue of Municipal Ordinance No. 9-2016. Allocation of budget was incorporated in the 2017 Annual Budget. The Office was filled up effective April of 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the municipal government agreed to set up its GAD Database.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality started setting up the GAD Database as a result of the CBMS survey. The GAD Focal Point System will initiate measures to consolidate available data to constitute GAD Database. As starting point, all data used for planning are sex-disaggregated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended management to enforce the immediate settlement of the disallowances amounting to P6,385,801.81.
observations.managementAction: The agency has partially complied with the recommendation. In addition, a case was filed with the Court for the collection of the disallowed amount.
observations.reason: Partial collection via payroll deduction was made during CY 2016 for a total of ₱109,443.90.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
a) Require the members of the SB to fully refund the amount of overpayment that should have been deducted from their RATA due to absences and failure to have the required number of sessions of at least four in a month as provided for in Section 52 of the Local Government Code of 1991; and,
b) Henceforth, claims for RATA should be supported by the SB minutes of meeting for the month and certification of attendance from the SB Secretary before payment. Claims of other officials receiving RATA should be supported with duly approved daily time records and application for leave.
observations.managementAction:
Partial collection was made as implementation of the recommendation. Verification of the Municipality’s accounts showed a settlement of the disallowances in the amount of ₱73,670.00.

Recommendation had been complied with. Supporting documents such as copy of the Minutes of Meetings and Certification of Attendance were attached to the claims for RATA.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management prepare the GAD Plan and allocate budget for the PAPs of the municipality and the GAD focal point person to prepare the plan in 2017 in accordance with the guidelines set forth by the Joint Circular No. 2004-01 to ensure that these PAPs are for the promotion of gender-responsive governance, fulfill women’s human rights and economic empowerment.
observations.managementAction:
A GAD Plan Budget Matrix was prepared as of 2016.

In addition, a GAD Plan was prepared based on the revised format.
